title: Ownership, Borrowing, Aliasing, and API Contracts · Undefined-Behavior Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted ownership-annotated buffer API, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in ownership-annotated buffer API and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify each pointer as unique owner, shared owner, mutable borrow, immutable borrow, weak observation, or non-owning handle.
Make ownership transfer conditional on observable success and leave failure states unambiguous.
Bound every borrow by owner lifetime, mutation rules, callback duration, and asynchronous retention.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Ownership Table Review
steps.instruction:
Order the Ownership Table Review evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Ownership Table Review.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Ownership Table Review assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Ownership Table Review, keep Ownership Table Review connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.text: A debug native-systems team handles memory case 9682. Produce bounded C evidence to track owner, borrow, mutation, and release state; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Review public APIs with an ownership table covering inputs, outputs, globals, nested allocations, and cleanup.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Ownership Table Review, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: A destructor alone makes a multi-allocation API ownership-complete.
